N0.name: Sunset
N0.detail: The star's position at sunset. At this time, only the brightest stars or planets, such as Venus and Jupiter, may be visible to the naked eye.

N1.name: Civil dusk ends
N1.detail: The star's position when the sun is 6 degrees below the horizon after sunset. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 1 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

N2.name: Nautical dusk ends
N2.detail: The star's position when the sun is 12 degrees below the horizon after sunset. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 4 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

N3.name: Astronomical dusk ends
N3.detail: The star's position when the sun is 18 degrees below the horizon after sunset. Astronomical observation and astrophotography typically begin after this time.

D3.name: Astronomical dawn starts
D3.detail: The star's position when the sun is 18 degrees below the horizon before sunrise. Astronomical observation and astrophotography typically take place before this time.

D2.name: Nautical dawn starts
D2.detail: The star's position when the sun is 12 degrees below the horizon before sunrise. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 4 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

D1.name: Civil dawn starts
D1.detail: The star's position when the sun is 6 degrees below the horizon before sunrise. At this time, stars brighter than magnitude 1 are visible to the naked eye in most parts of the sky.

D0.name: Sunrise
D0.detail: The star's position at sunrise. At this time, only the brightest stars or planets, such as Venus and Jupiter, may be visible to the naked eye.

R.name: Rising
R.detail: The position where the star appears to rise above the horizon.

T.name: Meridian transit
T.detail: The position where the star transits the meridian.

S.name: Setting
S.detail: The position where the star appears to dip below the horizon.

line_legend.solid: The path in the nighttime sky.
line_legend.dotted: The path in the daytime sky.

no_dst: Standard Time here reflects the current local time zone, without Daylight Saving Time (DST) adjustment.
